mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction sector activity, which compounds corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals strengthening corporate output and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP reflect broad economic expansion and higher corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals that higher borrowing costs are successfully suppressing manufacturing activity, which reduces near-term earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of cases.
